task_completion.description: Verify the conditions CSV file is created with the required columns and 12 rows.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet editor, text editor, or terminal output showing the contents of 'fitts_targets.csv' with headers 'target_w', 'target_amp', 'direction', and 'pos_x', and exactly 12 rows of data.

task_completion.description: Verify the PsychoPy experiment is saved to the correct path and uses 'height' units.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Builder window showing the experiment name 'fitts_task' in the title bar or file tree, and the Experiment Settings dialog showing 'height' selected for units.

task_completion.description: Verify a central gray home position stimulus is created with size 0.04.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Polygon properties window showing a stimulus (e.g., home) with color set to gray, size set to 0.04 (or [0.04, 0.04]), and position set to (0, 0).

task_completion.description: Verify a red target stimulus is created with size set to $target_w and position set to ($pos_x, 0).
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Polygon properties window showing a stimulus with color set to red, size set to '$target_w' (or [$target_w, $target_w]), position set to '($pos_x, 0)', and these fields set to 'set every repeat'.

task_completion.description: Verify a Mouse response component is added to advance the routine on click and record timing data.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Mouse properties window showing 'End Routine on press' enabled (e.g., valid clicks or any click) and 'Save mouse state' or time recording enabled.

task_completion.description: Verify a loop is wrapped around the routine and linked to the conditions CSV file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: PsychoPy Loop properties window showing the 'Conditions' field pointing to 'fitts_targets.csv' and the summary indicating 12 conditions/trials.

integrity.description: The agent used the PsychoPy Builder GUI to construct the experiment.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing interaction with the PsychoPy Builder interface (adding routines, components, loops) rather than just writing an XML/Psyexp file via a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent generated the CSV file using a script, spreadsheet software, or manual entry.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing python code, a spreadsheet application, or terminal commands used to create and populate the CSV, rather than downloading a pre-existing file.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the component property dialogs in PsychoPy.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing open dialog boxes for Polygon, Mouse, and Loop components with values being actively entered.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The experiment must be saved at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/fitts_law/fitts_task.psyexp'. The conditions CSV must be saved at '/home/ga/PsychoPyExperiments/fitts_law/conditions/fitts_targets.csv'. The CSV must contain exactly 12 rows of data (excluding headers), generated from combinations of amplitudes [0.4, 0.6, 0.8], widths [0.05, 0.1], and directions [-1, 1]. The 'pos_x' column values must be calculated as '(target_amp / 2) * direction' (resulting in values like 0.2, -0.2, 0.3, -0.3, 0.4, -0.4).
